# SSP / bidder parameters — Magnite Demand Manager → Assertive Yield migration

**Status:** reference snapshot for the planned Prebid Server cutover.
**Source of truth:** the two Magnite Demand Manager "Server Patterns" exports
provided by the yield team —
`DemandManagerWeb_ServerNewsweek_Desktop_ServerPatterns.xlsx` and
`...Mobile_ServerPatterns.xlsx` (archived under
`docs/assertive_yield/source_exports/`). This doc is a faithful, machine-parsed
transcription of those workbooks so the full S2S bidder roster and every
per-ad-unit parameter survives the move off Magnite.

> ⚠️ **Migration intent.** Newsweek is moving its **server-side (S2S / Prebid
> Server) header-bidding** stack from **Magnite Demand Manager** (`mgnipbs` —
> Magnite's hosted Prebid Server) to **Assertive Yield's Prebid Server**. The
> Magnite admin console disappears at contract termination, so every bidder's
> account-level credentials and per-ad-unit placement IDs below must be
> re-entered into the Assertive Yield Prebid Server config **before** we turn
> Magnite off. Treat this file as the porting checklist.

## What this covers

These exports describe the **Prebid Server (S2S) demand configuration only** —
the set of SSP/exchange "bidders" Magnite calls on Newsweek's behalf, keyed by
ad unit. It does **not** include client-side Prebid.js adapters, GAM line-item
setup, price-granularity/timeout settings, or the identity/UserID module config
(those live elsewhere in Demand Manager and should be inventoried separately
before cutover — see the checklist at the bottom).

Two platforms, each with its own ad-unit list and (mostly) its own placement
IDs:

- **Desktop** — 12 display ad units + 1 video unit.
- **Mobile** — 12 display ad units + 1 video unit.

Each bidder is configured per **channel**:

- **`puc`** = display/banner (and native) demand.
- **`vast`** = video (VAST) demand.

A bidder may run on one or both channels and on one or both platforms (see the
roster). Within a channel, some parameters are **account-level constants**
(identical across every ad unit — e.g. a publisher ID) and some are
**per-ad-unit** (a distinct placement/zone/tag ID per slot). The per-bidder
sections below split them out accordingly; **partial-coverage** bidders (not
wired to every slot) are flagged with ⚠️.

## Bidder roster ({n_bidders} bidders)

`mgnipbs` is the Magnite Prebid Server module these patterns belong to. Bidder
keys are Magnite's; the middle column maps each to the exchange and its likely
Prebid bidder code (verify the exact `bidder` string against Assertive Yield's
Prebid Server build before porting — some differ, e.g. `rubicon`→`magnite`,
`yahooAds`→`yahoossp`).

| Magnite bidder key | Exchange / notes | Desktop display | Desktop video | Mobile display | Mobile video |
|---|---|:--:|:--:|:--:|:--:|
{roster_tbl}

## Account-level IDs at a glance (the credentials to port first)

These seller/publisher-account identifiers are constant across slots and are the
"who we are" half of each integration — they normally come from the SSP, not
from Magnite, so confirm each with the partner during cutover. Exact per-ad-unit
values are in the detail tables below; these are only the shared/constant ones.

- **rubicon (Magnite):** `accountId` 9619 · desktop `siteId` 500456 · mobile `siteId` 500452
- **pubmatic:** `publisherId` 156850
- **openx:** `delDomain` ibt-d.openx.net (per-slot `unit`)
- **appnexus:** per-slot `placementId` only
- **yahooAds:** desktop display `dcn` 8a969c01018e8e7f637b80cad0000001 · mobile display `dcn` 8a9695c6018e8e80c901815f47710002 · video `dcn` 8a9699c6017d7d047ddc05159501002a
- **undertone:** `publisherId` 4083
- **medianet:** `cid` 8CUR8943F
- **zeta_global_ssp:** `sid` 679 · `shortname` franklymedia (reseller path)
- **onetag:** `pubId` 770185590958b74
- **minutemedia:** `org` 01htjtt9m5zw
- **imds:** `seatId` Newsweek_display / Newsweek_video (desktop), newsweek_display / newsweek_video (mobile)
- **insticator:** `publisherId` 12f5dd4d-6c1e-46c8-a5e0-2596cce735c5
- **vidazoo:** `pId` 59ac17c192832d0011283fe3 · display `cId` 66c334fb4a8ea7ce7ab93074 · video `cId` 6730af3c3f02d1bae3b460b5
- **ogury:** `assetKey` OGY-597397509A6D
- **sovrn:** `tagid` 1284710 (display) / 1284711 (video)
- **sparteo:** `networkId` c77c2ec1-00fe-4339-964f-9f37b8d7734c
- **oms:** `publisherId` 21186 (display) / 211861 desktop-video / 211862 mobile-video
- **openweb:** `org` sp_vNTS7w0J
- **smartadserver (Equativ):** `networkId` 5429
- **amx:** `tagId` nxGehxNK1
- **inmobi (mobile only):** per-slot `plc`
- **seedtag:** `publisherId` 0188-9567-01
- **mobkoi (mobile only):** `placementId` A7AAEBED · `adServerBaseUrl` https://pbs.mobkoi.com/bid/prebidserver

## Migration checklist (Magnite Demand Manager → Assertive Yield Prebid Server)

- [ ] **Confirm bidder codes.** Map each Magnite bidder key above to the exact
      Prebid Server `bidder` string in Assertive Yield's PBS build (e.g.
      `rubicon`→`magnite`, `yahooAds`→`yahoossp`). A wrong code silently drops
      the bidder.
- [ ] **Port account-level IDs** (the "at a glance" list) into Assertive Yield's
      config for every bidder/channel/platform combination.
- [ ] **Port per-ad-unit IDs** (placementId / zoneId / tagid / adSlot / unit /
      siteId / pos / inventoryCode / crid …) from the per-bidder tables. These
      are slot-specific; a copy/paste error mis-routes demand.
- [ ] **Recreate the ad-unit taxonomy.** 12 display + 1 video per platform; sizes
      are 970x250 (top), 728x90 (sticky desktop) / 300x50+320x50 (sticky mobile),
      300x250 (in-article), and the video unit.
- [ ] **Re-establish seller relationships / re-sign sellers.json + ads.txt.** Some
      lines run through resellers (e.g. Zeta via `franklymedia`); the new PBS host
      may need its own ads.txt / sellers.json entries before bids clear.
- [ ] **Resolve partial-coverage gaps** (⚠️ rows): decide whether medianet
      (desktop in-article9), seedtag, mobkoi, etc. should be extended to all
      slots or intentionally left partial.
- [ ] **Inventory the config NOT in these exports** before cutover: price
      granularity, auction/bidder timeouts, floors (the `floor`/`bidFloor`
      columns are empty here — confirm floor strategy moves too), the UserID /
      identity modules, currency, and any per-bidder `bidonmultiformat` /
      `testMode` flags.
- [ ] **Validate with a test wrapper** on Assertive Yield PBS (compare bid
      responses per bidder/slot against current Magnite delivery) before flipping
      production traffic.
- [ ] **Decommission Magnite** only after Assertive Yield is verified live —
      keep this snapshot until then.
